text.title: Design Doc Examples Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Doc Examples. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Maintain exactly four worked examples: single-service feature, cross-service refactor, data migration, hot-path performance.
rule.rationale: Four archetypes cover ~85% of design problems without overwhelming the writer.

rule.statement: Every example is fully filled in with realistic content — no placeholders, no placeholders inside the example body.
rule.rationale: Placeholders signal 'optional' and writers skip the sections; full content sets the expected depth.

rule.statement: Examples carry the same version as design-doc-structure; updates to structure trigger re-syncing examples in the same PR.
rule.rationale: Drift between structure and examples confuses writers.

rule.statement: Writer states which archetype they picked in the final doc's front-matter; 'none' is a valid value but must be intentional.
rule.rationale: Stating the archetype lets reviewers calibrate expectations.

rule.statement: Writer adapts: every example field is either replaced with real content or explicitly marked 'kept from example'.
rule.rationale: Verbatim copies signal lack of engagement; the archetype tag tracks adapted vs kept.

rule.statement: Examples are reviewed quarterly to keep them aligned with current architecture and tooling.
rule.rationale: Stale examples mislead writers; quarterly review keeps them fresh.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-doc-examples.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
